items.title: OpenAI raises $110B in one of the largest private funding rounds in history

content.abstract: OpenAI has raised $110 billion in a deal valued at $730-840 billion, with Amazon ($50B), Nvidia ($30B), and SoftBank ($30B) as key investors. Largest private financing in history. OpenAI also expanding AWS agreement by $100B over 8 years.

items.title: ChatGPT reaches 900M weekly active users

content.abstract: ChatGPT now has over 900 million weekly active users, 50 million consumer subscribers, and 9 million paying business users. 350% growth in 18 months. January and February 2026 on track to be largest subscriber months.

items.title: Pentagon moves to designate Anthropic as a supply-chain risk

content.abstract: Defense Secretary Hegseth directed Pentagon to designate Anthropic as supply-chain risk to national security — first ever such designation for an American company. Anthropic refused to lift AI safety guardrails for military use. Anthropic will challenge in court.

items.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman announces Pentagon deal with 'technical safeguards'

content.abstract: OpenAI announced deal with Pentagon to deploy AI models in classified military networks, immediately after Anthropic was designated supply chain risk. OpenAI claims 'technical safeguards' will be maintained.

items.title: Why China's humanoid robot industry is winning the early market

content.abstract: China dominates humanoid robot market: 87-90% of 13,000+ robots shipped globally in 2025 made by Chinese companies. Unitree sold 5,500 units. Industry expected to surpass 20B yuan ($2.8B) by 2026. Key challenge: 2-3 hour battery life.

items.title: AI music generator Suno hits 2M paid subscribers and $300M in annual recurring revenue

content.abstract: Suno AI music platform reaches 2 million paid subscribers and $300M ARR, demonstrating rapid commercialization of generative AI in creative industries.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom

content.abstract: $3 trillion total data center build-out needed for AI boom. US construction starts reached $77.7B in 2025 (190% YoY increase). 76 projects worth $88B planned for next 6 months. Major deals include xAI $20B Mississippi data center, Nvidia $2B CoreWeave investment.

items.title: India disrupts access to popular developer platform Supabase with blocking order

content.abstract: India blocks access to Supabase, a popular open-source developer platform, reflecting broader trend of national internet controls and tech sovereignty.

items.title: After Zomato, Deepinder Goyal returns with a $54M brain-monitoring bet

content.abstract: Zomato founder Deepinder Goyal invests $54M in brain-monitoring technology, signaling convergence of tech entrepreneurship and neurotechnology.

items.title: Iran attacks and retaliatory strikes 'undermine international peace and security'

content.abstract: UN Secretary-General Guterres and UN agency heads condemned joint Israeli and US attacks on Iran (Operation Roaring Lion/Epic Fury). 500 military targets struck with 200 fighter jets. Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei reportedly killed. Iran retaliates with strikes on US military bases in Gulf states. Major geopolitical crisis with global oil market implications.

items.title: UN unveils bold overhaul of strained humanitarian system

content.abstract: General Assembly briefed on UN80 Initiative reforms for humanitarian system improvements, reflecting institutional adaptation to mounting global crises.

items.title: Guterres slams international failure to manage safe migration

content.abstract: UN Secretary-General calls for implementation of 2018 migration cooperation agreement, highlighting global failure on safe migration management.

items.title: Recommendations for influenza vaccine composition for the 2026-2027 northern hemisphere season

content.abstract: WHO releases influenza vaccine composition recommendations for 2026-2027 northern hemisphere season, guiding global vaccine production.

items.title: White House stalls release of approved US science budgets

content.abstract: White House delays release of approved US science budgets, creating uncertainty for federal research funding and scientific institutions.

items.title: Is a 'selfish gene' making a Utah family have twice as many boys as girls?

content.abstract: Scientific investigation into potential selfish gene mechanism causing unusual sex ratio in Utah family, with implications for genetics and evolution.

items.title: How will we know if AI is smart enough to do science?

content.abstract: AAAS examination of benchmarks and criteria for evaluating whether AI systems can perform genuine scientific research, addressing the frontier of AI capabilities.

items.title: 'Truly spectacular' drug for sleeping sickness simplifies treatment, raising hopes for eradication

content.abstract: Acoziborole (Sanofi) approved by EMA — a single-dose (3 pills) treatment for sleeping sickness effective for both early and advanced stages. 95%+ cure rate at 18 months. Eliminates need for hospital visits and spinal taps. Sanofi donating doses to WHO.

items.title: Suspended small business research programs derail development of gene therapies, hip implants, and more

content.abstract: Suspension of US small business research programs (SBIR/STTR) disrupts development of gene therapies, medical devices, and other innovations.

items.title: AR is being brought to classrooms and hospitals

content.abstract: Augmented reality technology being applied in education and healthcare settings, demonstrating practical applications beyond entertainment.

items.title: AI is rewiring how the world's best Go players think

content.abstract: AI systems are fundamentally changing cognitive strategies of professional Go players, demonstrating how AI can augment rather than replace human expertise and reshape domain thinking.

items.title: Trump's fossil-fuel talk; Modi-Lula rare-earth pact; UK 'greenlash' question

content.abstract: Three key developments: Trump's fossil fuel policy talk, India-Brazil rare-earth minerals pact between Modi and Lula, and analysis of whether UK faces a 'greenlash' against climate policies.

items.title: The whole thing was a scam (Gary Marcus on AI industry critique)

content.abstract: Gary Marcus critiques the AI industry's trajectory and promises, questioning the fundamental value propositions being made by leading AI companies.

items.title: The Eternal Promise: A History of Attempts to Eliminate Programmers

content.abstract: Historical analysis of recurring attempts to eliminate programmers, from COBOL to modern AI coding tools, questioning whether current AI hype is different from past cycles.

items.title: Stop Burning Your Context Window - How We Cut MCP Output by 98% in Claude Code

content.abstract: Technical innovation in AI context window management achieving 98% reduction in MCP output, demonstrating ongoing efficiency improvements in AI engineering.

items.title: US and Israel launch major attack on Iran

content.abstract: Major military operation: US and Israel launch coordinated attack on Iran codenamed Operation Roaring Lion/Epic Fury. Iran's Supreme Leader reportedly killed. Retaliatory strikes on US bases in Gulf. Oil market shock expected.

items.title: There are no psychopaths

content.abstract: Essay challenging the concept of psychopathy, arguing that virtually everything known about psychopathy has been debunked, exploring why this discredited concept persists.

items.title: Markets brace for impact after US-Israel strikes on Iran

content.abstract: Global markets expected to face extreme volatility: equities down 1-2%+, US Treasury yields falling 5-10bps, oil jumping 5-10%. Nasdaq already posted worst February (-3%+) since March 2025. Canada Q4 GDP contracted 0.6%. Block's 40% workforce reduction seen as template for AI-driven corporate restructuring. Record February buyback authorizations of $233B.

items.title: Block's 40% workforce reduction as template for AI-driven corporate restructuring

content.abstract: Block's 40% workforce reduction seen as a template for AI-driven corporate restructuring in 2026. Companies are choosing capital return over headcount, with record February buyback authorizations of $233 billion.
